task_completion.description: The OpenBCI GUI is launched and the correct playback file is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenBCI GUI system control panel, 'PLAYBACK' mode is selected and the filename 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt' is visible as the data source.

task_completion.description: The dashboard displays two separate Band Power widgets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI dashboard layout contains at least two distinct panels or windows titled 'Band Power'.

task_completion.description: One Band Power widget is configured to display only Channel 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In one of the Band Power widgets, only Channel 1 is active. This is visually indicated by the presence of only Grey bars/data, or the channel selection toggles showing only '1' as selected.

task_completion.description: The second Band Power widget is configured to display only Channel 2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the other Band Power widget, only Channel 2 is active. This is visually indicated by the presence of only Purple bars/data, or the channel selection toggles showing only '2' as selected.

task_completion.description: The EEG data is actively streaming in the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GUI shows active data visualization (e.g., bars moving in the Band Power widgets, waveforms in the Time Series widget) and the system control button says 'Stop Data Stream', indicating it is currently running.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the dashboard is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window shows a screenshot command (e.g., scrot, import) targeting '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/asymmetry_setup.png', or a file manager window shows the 'asymmetry_setup.png' file successfully created in the correct directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine OpenBCI GUI interface being manipulated, not a mock-up, image editor, or text-based configuration file.

integrity.description: The agent captured a real screenshot rather than creating a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not simply use the 'touch' command to create an empty file named 'asymmetry_setup.png' or copy an unrelated image to bypass the requirement.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely configured the widgets in the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The channel toggles and widget dropdowns in the GUI reflect the required state, rather than the agent just opening default widgets and stopping.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required playback file is 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt'. In the OpenBCI GUI, Channel 1 is mapped to Grey (RGB 129, 129, 129) and Channel 2 is mapped to Purple (RGB 124, 75, 141). The expected screenshot must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/asymmetry_setup.png'.
